rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Notice Of Motion For An Amendment To The Complaint To Substitute The Names Of Angel Anders, Charles Charles And Freddie Fritz As Doe Defendants 1, 2, And 3
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion For An Amendment To The Complaint To Substitute The Names Of Angel Anders, Charles Charles And Freddie Fritz As Doe Defendants 1, 2, And 3. Plaintiff's motion to amend his complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiff alleges he was infected with HIV during a film shoot involving several dozen sexual participants in 2013. In recent depositions, plaintiff learned of three participants - Angel Anders, Charles Charles and Freddie Fritz - whom he now believes to be HIV-positive, and thus moves to name them as Doe defendants 1, 2 and 3. Defendants argue that plaintiff "unreasonably delayed substituting these three individuals into the action" because he had their names (with those of all film participants) in March 2017. However, the HIV claims are new information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NANCY DEVINE ET AL VS. DESTINATION HOTELS AND RESORTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendants Kimpton Group Holding, Llc, Kimpton Hotel And Restaurant Group, Llc, Khp Capital Partners, L.P. And Hospitality Investment Group, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT KIMPTON GROUP HOLDING, LLC, KIMPTON HOTEL & RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC, KHP CAPITAL PARTNERS, L.P., HOSPITALITY INVESTMENT GROUP, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Kimpton Group Holding, LLC, Kimpton Hotel & Restaurant Group, LLC, KHP Capital Partners, L.P. and Hospitality Investment Group, Inc. (calling themselves the "Kimpton entities") move for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. The case involves plaintiff's fall down a hotel staircase allegedly due to lack of a handrail required by building codes. The Kimpton entities sold the hotel in 2010, but continued to manage it until a month before the fall. The notice of motion fails to state its grounds, merely reciting the statutory summary judgment standard. From what I glean in the Kimpton entities' memorandum, they have essentially one argument: that "former owners/operators of property cease to be liable for dangerous conditions causing injury at the property upon the relinquishment of ownership and control, except under specified exceptions" set out in Preston v. Goldman (1986) 42 Cal.3d 108. Plaintiff opposes the motion by raising mixed issues of fact and law, at least two of which are well-taken. First, plaintiff argues that Preston does not apply to property which is opened to the general public, as the hotel staircase allegedly was. (See 42 Cal.3d at 116.) The Kimpton entities reply with Leakes v. Shamoun (1986) 187 Cal.App.3d 771, but plaintiff in that case was injured outside the property at issue. (Id.) At a minimum, material disputes exist over how open the hotel staircase was to the general public. (See, e.g., RPSS 34-36.) Second, one of Preston's exceptions is for "commercial builders, contractors or renovators," and the Kimpton entities are alleged to have renovated the hotel. (See 42 Cal. 3d n.10; RPSS 1-18.) The Kimpton entities argue the renovation's scope and that they are not professional renovators. Again, these are disputed issues of fact. The Kimpton entities' motion is DENIED in its entirety. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: 260 CLARA STREET HOA, A CALIFORNIA DOMESTIC VS. RICHARD HART ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT RICHARD HART, EAMONN HERLIHY, 260 CLARA STREET LLC, ROHAN, WALLACE AND AHERNE INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Richard Hart, Eamonn Herlihy, 260 Clara Street LLC and Rohan, Wallace and Aherne, Inc. move for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. One ground is asserted: that "each cause of action alleged" in the complaint "is barred by the statute of limitations set forth" in CCP §337.15. The motion is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to whether defendants engaged in willful misconduct. (See CCP §337.15(f); Acosta v. Glenfed Development Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1278.) Plaintiff adduces evidence that the latent construction defects were so egregious and obvious to a competent developer and constructor as to warrant an inference that defendants were aware of them and intentionally decided to construct the project in a substandard manner and/or proceeded with actionable lack of supervision over subcontractors. (See, e.g., Bigard Dec. 12; Buschovich Dec. 5, 6; Panasci Dec. 6, 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN KASEM VS. MICHAEL D. HUNTER MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Michael Hunter's demurrer urges the Court to resolve several factual disputes, largely based on judicial notice of out-of-court statements. That is not the proper role of a demurrer; rather, "plaintiff's allegations must be accepted as true." (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §7:44.) The demurrer is OVERRULED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Dr. Hunter is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANT OLDENBROOK IS GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS EXCEPT AS TO ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILIGE ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANT IS ORDER TO DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ASKED FOR IN REQUEST NOS. 1 - 40 FOR THE PERIODS SPECIFIED IN THE REQUESTS. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT OLDENBROOK IS WITHHOLDING ANY DOCUMENT ON THE GROUNDS OF A/C PRIVILEGE HE WILL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH A PRIVILEGE LOG WITH SUFFICIENT DETAIL TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
